mnemonics.title: ANOVA Mnemonic
mnemonics.content: ANOVA = Analysis Of Variance. Remember: 'Always Note Overall Variance Analysis' - it compares group means by analyzing variance.

mnemonics.title: Key Formula
mnemonics.content: SST = SSB + SSW. Total Sum of Squares = Between-groups Sum of Squares + Within-groups Sum of Squares. Think: 'Total = Between + Within'.

mnemonics.title: F-Statistic
mnemonics.content: F = MSB / MSW (Mean Square Between / Mean Square Within). A large F means groups are significantly different. Think: 'F for Finding differences'.

mnemonics.title: Three Assumptions
mnemonics.content: ANOVA requires INH: Independence of observations, Normality of data in each group, Homogeneity of variances across groups.

mnemonics.title: Decision Rule
mnemonics.content: If F_computed > F_critical at significance level alpha, reject H0 (means are not all equal). If F_computed <= F_critical, fail to reject H0.

mnemonics.title: Types of ANOVA
mnemonics.content: Four main types: One-way (1 factor), Two-way (2 factors), Latin-square (2 blocking variables), ANCOVA (with covariates). Remember: 'O-T-L-A'.
